text: Prediction
To promise
To guess
To expect
To doubt
Likely
Certain
Forecast
To wonder
To warn
Spontaneous
To offer
I think it will rain this afternoon.
I promise I will call you tonight.
That bag looks heavy. I'll help you.
Don't worry, I won't forget your birthday.
Hi Ana Paula! Did you check the weather forecast for our picnic on Saturday? I wonder if it will be a nice day.
I checked this morning. They say it will be warm and sunny, so I am certain the picnic will be great.
That is a relief. I doubt many people will come if it rains, so this is good news.
Exactly. I expect at least ten friends. If you need help with the food, I will bring a big salad, no problem.
Really? That is so kind. Thank you! I promise I will bring the drinks and the music.
Perfect. Oh, one warning: I think Chris will be late again, as always. But do not worry, it will be a wonderful afternoon.
I think it will be sunny this weekend.
Do not worry, I will help you.
I am certain it will be fine.
I doubt it will rain, but I will bring an umbrella.

People often ask me what I think the future will look like, and honestly, I love making predictions. I am certain that technology will change the way we work, and I expect that many of us will work from home much more. I doubt that big offices will completely disappear, but I think they will become smaller. The weather is harder to predict; even the best forecast is sometimes wrong. On a personal level, I promise myself one thing every January: I will learn something new. This year, it is a language. I am not sure I will speak it perfectly, but I am certain I will try my best. Nobody knows exactly what will happen next, and maybe that is the best part of the future.

Hi Ana Paula, it is Sarah! I just had a spontaneous idea and I had to call you. The weather forecast for Sunday looks perfect, so I wonder if you want to go to the beach with me. I promise it will be a relaxing day, with no work and no phones, just the sea. I doubt it will be crowded, because it is still early in the season. If you are tired, do not worry; I will drive, so you can just relax. I expect we will be back home by six. Oh, and I will bring lunch for both of us. Call me back when you can and tell me if you are free. I really think you will love it. Talk soon, bye!

First, start with what you notice or with the weather forecast. Then make your prediction using will. After that, say how certain you are, with 'I am certain' or 'I doubt'. Next, offer some help or make a promise with will. Finally, end with a friendly warning or a kind word.

In this traffic, I doubt they will arrive before noon.
